case.title: SAMSON MARKET CO., INC. v. KIRBY

opinion.h1: SAMSON MARKET CO., INC. v. KIRBY, 393 U.S. 12 (1968)
opinion.h4: SAMSON MARKET CO., INC. v. KIRBY, DIRECTOR, DEPARTMENT OF ALCOHOLIC BEVERAGE CONTROL OF CALIFORNIA. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEAL OF CALIFORNIA, SECOND APPELLATE DISTRICT. No. 181. Decided October 14, 1968.
opinion.p: Jacques Leslie and Lawrence Teplin for appellant.
Thomas C. Lynch, Attorney General of California, and Warren H. Deering and Henry G. Ullerich, Deputy Attorneys General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
393 U.S. 12 MARTONE v. MORGAN ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF LOUISIANA. No. 216. Decided October 14, 1968.
Ashton L. Stewart, Special Assistant Attorney General of Louisiana, and Wesley Wirtz, Assistant Attorney General, for appellees.
The appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
